{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Content and Publishing Manager",
  "description": "Detail and Avedia is an Integrated Business and Media Solutions Consulting agency dedicated to unlocking the potential of businesses across Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Content and Publishing Manager

Job Location: Costain/Iganmu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Content &amp; Publishing Manager is responsible for leading the end-to-end content lifecycle and managing the publishing operations of Detail and Avedia Limited.
	The role oversees content strategy, content creation, editorial quality, publishing, distribution, and performance reporting while ensuring alignment with client objectives and business goals.
	The role serves as the operational lead for the company&#39;s publishing arm, driving publishing revenue, managing book production, overseeing bookstore distribution, maintaining inventory accuracy, ensuring production quality, and expanding market reach for published works.
	The Content &amp; Publishing Manager collaborates with internal teams, external vendors, bookstores, distributors, authors, and clients to ensure efficient publishing operations and sustainable business growth.


Key Responsibilities
Publishing Business Management:


	Manage the day-to-day operations of the company&#39;s publishing arm.
	Develop and implement initiatives that contribute to publishing revenue growth and commercial success.
	Support the commercialization of published works through effective sales, distribution, and market expansion strategies.
	Identify opportunities for new publishing partnerships, collaborations, and revenue streams.
	Contribute to business development efforts that support the growth of the publishing division.


Book Production &amp; Quality Assurance:


	Oversee the production process for books, publications, educational materials, and other publishing projects.
	Coordinate with printers, production vendors, authors, editors, and designers to ensure timely delivery of projects.
	Establish and maintain quality control processes to ensure all publications meet company standards.
	Monitor production timelines and resolve issues that may affect delivery schedules or quality standards.
	Ensure all published materials are accurate, professionally presented, and fit for market distribution.


Distribution &amp; Bookstore Relationship Management:


	Build and maintain strong relationships with bookstores, distributors, educational institutions, and retail partners.
	Expand the company&#39;s distribution network to increase market reach and product availability.
	Coordinate book deliveries and monitor distribution turnaround times.
	Ensure published materials are available across targeted sales and distribution channels.
	Monitor distribution performance and implement initiatives to improve market penetration.


Inventory &amp; Consignment Management:


	Maintain accurate records of inventory, stock movement, and bookstore consignments.
	Monitor stock levels and coordinate replenishment where necessary.
	Ensure accurate reporting and reconciliation of consignment sales and inventory records.
	Work closely with Finance and Operations teams to maintain accountability and transparency in stock management.
	Conduct periodic inventory reviews and recommend improvements to stock management processes.


Governance, Compliance &amp; Operational Excellence:


	Ensure adherence to publishing standards, editorial guidelines, and company policies.
	Monitor service level agreements (SLAs) and ensure publishing requests are completed within agreed timelines.
	Identify operational risks, production bottlenecks, and distribution challenges and escalate them appropriately.
	Drive continuous improvement across content, publishing, and operational workflows.
	Maintain professionalism, accountability, and excellence across all responsibilities.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Mass Communication, Marketing, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 4 years&#39; progressive experience in publishing, content management, editorial operations, book production, or a related field.
	Experience working with printers, authors, editors, distributors, bookstores, educational institutions, and other publishing stakeholders.
	Experience driving business growth, revenue generation, partnerships, or market expansion initiatives.
	Experience within a publishing house, educational publishing company, media organization, marketing agency, or content-driven business will be an advantage.


Salary
N300,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: jobs@detailandavedia.com using &quot;Content &amp; Publishing Manager&quot; as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-15",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-23",

Content and Publishing Manager Job at Detail and Avedia




        
    

        
        Detail and Avedia is an Integrated Business and Media Solutions Consulting agency dedicated to unlocking the potential of businesses across Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Content and Publishing Manager

Job Location: Costain/Iganmu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Content & Publishing Manager is responsible for leading the end-to-end content lifecycle and managing the publishing operations of Detail and Avedia Limited.
	The role oversees content strategy, content creation, editorial quality, publishing, distribution, and performance reporting while ensuring alignment with client objectives and business goals.
	The role serves as the operational lead for the company's publishing arm, driving publishing revenue, managing book production, overseeing bookstore distribution, maintaining inventory accuracy, ensuring production quality, and expanding market reach for published works.
	The Content & Publishing Manager collaborates with internal teams, external vendors, bookstores, distributors, authors, and clients to ensure efficient publishing operations and sustainable business growth.


Key Responsibilities
Publishing Business Management:


	Manage the day-to-day operations of the company's publishing arm.
	Develop and implement initiatives that contribute to publishing revenue growth and commercial success.
	Support the commercialization of published works through effective sales, distribution, and market expansion strategies.
	Identify opportunities for new publishing partnerships, collaborations, and revenue streams.
	Contribute to business development efforts that support the growth of the publishing division.


Book Production & Quality Assurance:


	Oversee the production process for books, publications, educational materials, and other publishing projects.
	Coordinate with printers, production vendors, authors, editors, and designers to ensure timely delivery of projects.
	Establish and maintain quality control processes to ensure all publications meet company standards.
	Monitor production timelines and resolve issues that may affect delivery schedules or quality standards.
	Ensure all published materials are accurate, professionally presented, and fit for market distribution.


Distribution & Bookstore Relationship Management:


	Build and maintain strong relationships with bookstores, distributors, educational institutions, and retail partners.
	Expand the company's distribution network to increase market reach and product availability.
	Coordinate book deliveries and monitor distribution turnaround times.
	Ensure published materials are available across targeted sales and distribution channels.
	Monitor distribution performance and implement initiatives to improve market penetration.


Inventory & Consignment Management:


	Maintain accurate records of inventory, stock movement, and bookstore consignments.
	Monitor stock levels and coordinate replenishment where necessary.
	Ensure accurate reporting and reconciliation of consignment sales and inventory records.
	Work closely with Finance and Operations teams to maintain accountability and transparency in stock management.
	Conduct periodic inventory reviews and recommend improvements to stock management processes.


Governance, Compliance & Operational Excellence:


	Ensure adherence to publishing standards, editorial guidelines, and company policies.
	Monitor service level agreements (SLAs) and ensure publishing requests are completed within agreed timelines.
	Identify operational risks, production bottlenecks, and distribution challenges and escalate them appropriately.
	Drive continuous improvement across content, publishing, and operational workflows.
	Maintain professionalism, accountability, and excellence across all responsibilities.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor's Degree in Mass Communication, Marketing, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 4 years' progressive experience in publishing, content management, editorial operations, book production, or a related field.
	Experience working with printers, authors, editors, distributors, bookstores, educational institutions, and other publishing stakeholders.
	Experience driving business growth, revenue generation, partnerships, or market expansion initiatives.
	Experience within a publishing house, educational publishing company, media organization, marketing agency, or content-driven business will be an advantage.


Salary
N300,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Content & Publishing Manager" as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Virtual Assistant & Operations Admin",
  "description": "At Smithstone Properties, we specialize in Hospitality Management, Property Management, and Property Development&mdash;the three pillars of our business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Virtual Assistant &amp; Operations Admin

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time (In &ndash; Person (Mon &ndash; Fri), Remote (Weekend)
Start Date: Immediate
Working Hours: 8 AM &ndash; 5 PM (Weekends &amp; after-hours on-call for guest emergencies)

Role Overview


	This role blends virtual assistance, operations administration, and property management support.
	You will be responsible for managing long term tenants, serviced accommodation guest communications, overseeing check-ins, and continuously improving processes to enhance efficiency.


Key Responsibilities
Tenant Management &ndash; Admin Responsibilities:


	Advertising properties on approved platforms
	Managing advert enquiries and booking viewings
	Coordinating viewings with team members
	Issuing tenant application forms and contracts
	Supporting referencing &amp; Right to Rent checks
	Managing tenant communications (day-to-day queries)
	Logging and coordinating maintenance with contractors
	Scheduling access for repairs
	Monitoring rent payments and sending reminders
	Managing renewals and notices
	Updating tenant/property records
	Preparing end-of-month reports per property.


Admin Responsibilities:


	Provide virtual administrative support, including scheduling, email management, and data entry.
	Utilise PowerPoint and advanced Excel to create reports, presentations, and track key property data.
	Identify and implement process improvements (eligible for a &pound;50 bonus per approved improvement)
	Maintain property management records and update relevant systems as needed.
	Assist in general operational support for property and serviced accommodation management.


Guest Communication &amp; Support:


	Monitor and respond promptly to guest messages across all booking platforms (Airbnb, Booking.com, Vrbo, direct bookings) and phone inquiries.
	Provide accurate information regarding check-in, check-out, property features, and house rules.
	Handle guest inquiries, complaints, and special requests professionally and efficiently.
	Call guests if noise sensors are triggered to ensure neighbors are not disturbed and take appropriate action if necessary.
	Manage the guest onboarding process, including ID verification, check-in instructions, and security deposit where applicable.


Booking &amp; Operational Coordination:


	Oversee the entire guest journey, from reservation confirmation to check-out, ensuring a seamless experience.
	Coordinate with key collection partners to ensure keys are dropped off in the lockbox before guest arrival.
	Align with cleaning teams to adjust schedules when guests purchase late check-out or extended stays.
	Communicate with property managers or directors regarding any special guest requests that require approval or additional services.


Guest Reviews &amp; Reputation Management:


	Leave timely and professional reviews for guests on booking platforms.
	Monitor and respond to guest reviews, addressing any concerns and highlighting positive feedback.


What We&rsquo;re Looking For


	Minimum of 5 years of experience in administrative, operations, or property management roles.
	Strong verbal communication and interpersonal skills.
	Hospitality management experience is a plus.
	Basic knowledge of cloud-based tools and virtual workspaces.
	Strong team-working skills and ability to collaborate effectively.
	Ability to work under pressure and handle multiple responsibilities efficiently.
	A proactive individual with a keen interest in learning and improving processes.
	Passionate about the hospitality industry and delivering top-tier customer service.
	Proficiency in PowerPoint and advanced Excel (pivot tables, data analysis, reporting).
	Highly organized with excellent problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work independently and efficiently in a remote setting.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary - N350,000 monthly
	Training budget to support your professional growth
	&pound;50 incentive for each approved process improvement idea
	Opportunity to work in a growing property management company
	A dynamic, collaborative, and innovative work environment
	Other incentives - training budget &amp; from #100,000 process improvement incentive.


Method of Application

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to: contact@smithstoneproperties.co.uk using the Job Position as the subejct of the mail.",

Virtual Assistant & Operations Admin Job at Smithstone Propertie




        
    

        
        At Smithstone Properties, we specialize in Hospitality Management, Property Management, and Property Development—the three pillars of our business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Virtual Assistant & Operations Admin

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time (In – Person (Mon – Fri), Remote (Weekend)
Start Date: Immediate
Working Hours: 8 AM – 5 PM (Weekends & after-hours on-call for guest emergencies)

Role Overview

	This role blends virtual assistance, operations administration, and property management support.
	You will be responsible for managing long term tenants, serviced accommodation guest communications, overseeing check-ins, and continuously improving processes to enhance efficiency.


Key Responsibilities
Tenant Management – Admin Responsibilities:


	Advertising properties on approved platforms
	Managing advert enquiries and booking viewings
	Coordinating viewings with team members
	Issuing tenant application forms and contracts
	Supporting referencing & Right to Rent checks
	Managing tenant communications (day-to-day queries)
	Logging and coordinating maintenance with contractors
	Scheduling access for repairs
	Monitoring rent payments and sending reminders
	Managing renewals and notices
	Updating tenant/property records

Admin Responsibilities:

	Identify and implement process improvements (eligible for a £50 bonus per approved improvement)
	Maintain property management records and update relevant systems as needed.
	Assist in general operational support for property and serviced accommodation management.


Guest Communication & Support:


	Monitor and respond promptly to guest messages across all booking platforms (Airbnb, Booking.com, Vrbo, direct bookings) and phone inquiries.
	Provide accurate information regarding check-in, check-out, property features, and house rules.
	Handle guest inquiries, complaints, and special requests professionally and efficiently.
	Call guests if noise sensors are triggered to ensure neighbors are not disturbed and take appropriate action if necessary.
	Manage the guest onboarding process, including ID verification, check-in instructions, and security deposit where applicable.


Booking & Operational Coordination:


	Oversee the entire guest journey, from reservation confirmation to check-out, ensuring a seamless experience.
	Coordinate with key collection partners to ensure keys are dropped off in the lockbox before guest arrival.
	Align with cleaning teams to adjust schedules when guests purchase late check-out or extended stays.
	Communicate with property managers or directors regarding any special guest requests that require approval or additional services.


Guest Reviews & Reputation Management:


	Leave timely and professional reviews for guests on booking platforms.
	Monitor and respond to guest reviews, addressing any concerns and highlighting positive feedback.


What We’re Looking For


	Minimum of 5 years of experience in administrative, operations, or property management roles.
	Strong verbal communication and interpersonal skills.
	Hospitality management experience is a plus.
	Basic knowledge of cloud-based tools and virtual workspaces.
	Strong team-working skills and ability to collaborate effectively.
	Ability to work under pressure and handle multiple responsibilities efficiently.
	A proactive individual with a keen interest in learning and improving processes.
	Passionate about the hospitality industry and delivering top-tier customer service.
	Proficiency in PowerPoint and advanced Excel (pivot tables, data analysis, reporting).
	Highly organized with excellent problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work independently and efficiently in a remote setting.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary - N350,000 monthly
	Training budget to support your professional growth
	£50 incentive for each approved process improvement idea
	Opportunity to work in a growing property management company
	A dynamic, collaborative, and innovative work environment
	Other incentives - training budget & from #100,000 process improvement incentive.


Method of Application

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subejct of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Hotel Operations Supervisor",
  "description": "Welcome to Teestone Hotel &amp; Apartments, a modern boutique hotel located in the peaceful neighborhood of Ikeja and only 15 minutes from Muritala Muhammed International Airport.

For travelers searching for hotels in Ikeja, Teestone offers the perfect combination of comfort, convenience, and contemporary hospitality. Whether you are visiting Lagos for business, attending meetings in Ikeja, catching an early flight, or simply planning a relaxing staycation, our hotel provides a stylish and welcoming environment designed to make your stay memorable.

Unlike many traditional hotels near Lagos airport, Teestone Hotel &amp; Apartments provides guests with a quieter and more relaxing setting away from the heavy airport traffic while still maintaining quick and easy access to the airport and major commercial areas of Ikeja.

Our thoughtfully designed rooms, rooftop bar, suspended swimming pool, and personalized guest experience make Teestone one of the most comfortable places to stay for visitors looking for quality accommodation in Ikeja.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Hotel Operations Supervisor
Job Location: Ojodu Berger, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a dedicated and experienced Hotel Operations Supervisor to oversee the daily operations of the hotel and ensure exceptional guest service.
	The successful candidate will coordinate and supervise front office, housekeeping, food and beverage, maintenance, and security teams to maintain high operational standards, guest satisfaction, and efficient hotel performance.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and coordinate daily hotel operations across all departments.
	Ensure guests receive excellent service and promptly resolve complaints or concerns.
	Monitor staff performance and ensure adherence to hotel policies and procedures.
	Assist in scheduling staff shifts and maintaining adequate staffing levels.
	Conduct regular inspections of guest rooms, public areas, and hotel facilities to ensure cleanliness and quality standards.
	Coordinate with department heads to ensure smooth communication and workflow.
	Monitor inventory levels and report operational needs or shortages.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and security regulations.
	Prepare operational reports and provide updates to hotel management.
	Assist in staff training, onboarding, and performance evaluations.
	Support revenue-generating activities and help achieve hotel occupancy and service targets.
	Handle emergency situations and ensure appropriate corrective actions are taken.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree, HND, OND, or equivalent qualification in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Previous experience in hotel operations, hospitality supervision, or a similar role.
	Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.
	Excellent problem-solving and decision-making abilities.
	Ability to work flexible hours, including weekends and holidays.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and hotel management software is an advantage.
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to: teestonejobs@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-22",

      "addressCountry": "NG"

  },

    }

}



Hotel Operations Supervisor Job at Teestone Hotel & Apartments




        
    

        
        Welcome to Teestone Hotel & Apartments, a modern boutique hotel located in the peaceful neighborhood of Ikeja and only 15 minutes from Muritala Muhammed International Airport.

For travelers searching for hotels in Ikeja, Teestone offers the perfect combination of comfort, convenience, and contemporary hospitality. Whether you are visiting Lagos for business, attending meetings in Ikeja, catching an early flight, or simply planning a relaxing staycation, our hotel provides a stylish and welcoming environment designed to make your stay memorable.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Unlike many traditional hotels near Lagos airport, Teestone Hotel & Apartments provides guests with a quieter and more relaxing setting away from the heavy airport traffic while still maintaining quick and easy access to the airport and major commercial areas of Ikeja.

Our thoughtfully designed rooms, rooftop bar, suspended swimming pool, and personalized guest experience make Teestone one of the most comfortable places to stay for visitors looking for quality accommodation in Ikeja.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Hotel Operations Supervisor
Job Location: Ojodu Berger, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a dedicated and experienced Hotel Operations Supervisor to oversee the daily operations of the hotel and ensure exceptional guest service.
	The successful candidate will coordinate and supervise front office, housekeeping, food and beverage, maintenance, and security teams to maintain high operational standards, guest satisfaction, and efficient hotel performance.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and coordinate daily hotel operations across all departments.
	Ensure guests receive excellent service and promptly resolve complaints or concerns.
	Monitor staff performance and ensure adherence to hotel policies and procedures.
	Assist in scheduling staff shifts and maintaining adequate staffing levels.
	Conduct regular inspections of guest rooms, public areas, and hotel facilities to ensure cleanliness and quality standards.
	Coordinate with department heads to ensure smooth communication and workflow.
	Monitor inventory levels and report operational needs or shortages.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and security regulations.
	Prepare operational reports and provide updates to hotel management.
	Assist in staff training, onboarding, and performance evaluations.
	Support revenue-generating activities and help achieve hotel occupancy and service targets.
	Handle emergency situations and ensure appropriate corrective actions are taken.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree, HND, OND, or equivalent qualification in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Previous experience in hotel operations, hospitality supervision, or a similar role.
	Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.
	Excellent problem-solving and decision-making abilities.
	Ability to work flexible hours, including weekends and holidays.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and hotel management software is an advantage.
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "CMP Nigeria Limited is a construction project management company in Lagos. Our services include project management, construction management, and site development, all delivered with innovation, sustainability, and excellence.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer

Job Location: Dolphin Estate Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are seeking a Front Desk Officer to be the first point of contact for our organization.
	The ideal candidate will be personable, organized, and able to manage multiple responsibilities while maintaining a professional demeanor.


Key Responsibilities


	Greet and assist visitors, clients, and staff in a courteous manner
	Manage incoming calls, emails, and correspondence
	Maintain reception area and ensure it reflects the company&rsquo;s professional image
	Schedule appointments and manage meeting room bookings
	Provide administrative support to various departments
	Handle inquiries and direct them to appropriate personnel


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND / Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in any discipline
	Proven experience in a front desk, administrative, or customer service role
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
	Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)
	Professional appearance and attitude
	Must reside close to Dolphin Estate Ikoyi, Lagos.


Salary
N80,000 - N100,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;

Front Desk Officer Job at CMP Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        CMP Nigeria Limited is a construction project management company in Lagos. Our services include project management, construction management, and site development, all delivered with innovation, sustainability, and excellence.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Officer

Job Location: Dolphin Estate Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a Front Desk Officer to be the first point of contact for our organization.
	The ideal candidate will be personable, organized, and able to manage multiple responsibilities while maintaining a professional demeanor.


Key Responsibilities


	Greet and assist visitors, clients, and staff in a courteous manner
	Manage incoming calls, emails, and correspondence
	Maintain reception area and ensure it reflects the company’s professional image
	Schedule appointments and manage meeting room bookings
	Provide administrative support to various departments
	Handle inquiries and direct them to appropriate personnel


Requirements


	Minimum of OND / HND / Bachelor’s Degree in any discipline
	Proven experience in a front desk, administrative, or customer service role
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
	Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)
	Professional appearance and attitude
	Must reside close to Dolphin Estate Ikoyi, Lagos.


Salary
N80,000 - N100,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Location: Maryland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking highly motivated, experienced and result driven Electrical Design Engineer to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for developing technical designs and drawings while also supporting the commercial and bidding aspects of engineering projects in line with the company&#39;s objective.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess BSc / HND in Electrical Engineering, or a related field.
	1 - 2 years of proven experience in Engineering and Commercial design.
	Proficiency in AutoCAD, Revit and other relevant design tools
	Good knowledge in MEP and ELVS solution.
	Good knowledge on costing, budgeting and commercial evaluation.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


Salary

Method of Application&nbsp;

Electrical Design Engineer Job at Basscomm Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        BASSCOMM is a Mechanical and Electrical Contractor working throughout Nigeria with a reputation for completing projects efficiently, within time and budget constraints and to the highest quality it has made us the Contractor of choice for our industry over the past decade. We currently operate across Nigeria in various sectors throughout the industry.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Design Engineer

Job Location: Maryland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking highly motivated, experienced and result driven Electrical Design Engineer to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for developing technical designs and drawings while also supporting the commercial and bidding aspects of engineering projects in line with the company's objective.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess BSc / HND in Electrical Engineering, or a related field.
	1 - 2 years of proven experience in Engineering and Commercial design.
	Proficiency in AutoCAD, Revit and other relevant design tools
	Good knowledge in MEP and ELVS solution.
	Good knowledge on costing, budgeting and commercial evaluation.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only Qualified candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply
